events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hazard: shims missing under base plate during column/beam erection & plumb. Need identify safety hazards, severity, immediate controls. Decision fail because non-compliant and potentially dangerous. Risk high? Missing shims under base plate can cause column instability, uneven bearing, load transfer issues, potential collapse during erection. Severity high/critical? Since actively in progress, column may not be plumb/stable. Base plate not properly shimmed leads to inadequate bearing and leveling; column could shift or settle. In industrial site, high risk. Decision fail. Risk high maybe critical? Let's decide high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 60? Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Findings: shims missing under base plate; column base not level/bearing properly; load not transferred per design; potential for column shift/settlement and collapse. Actions: stop work, install specified shims per drawings/AISC, verify level/plumb and bearing, inspect by competent person/PE, do not load until corrected. References: AISC 303 (base plate bearing, shimming, leveling), OSHA 1926 Subpart R (column erection stability, fall protection? maybe 1926.755 column anchorage). Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Shims are missing under the column base plate.",
    "Base plate is not properly leveled and lacks full bearing on the foundation.",
    "Column load path is not installed per the engineered details, creating instability.",
    "Risk of column settlement, shift, or overturning during erection/plumbing."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work in the affected area and secure the column against movement.",
    "Install the specified shims under the base plate per the engineered drawings and AISC 303.",
    "Re-check base plate level, plumb, and full bearing contact before releasing the column.",
    "Have a competent person or qualified inspector verify the correction before resuming loading/erection."
  ],
  "rationale": "Missing base-plate shims create an unstable, non-compliant column installation with inadequate bearing and leveling, which can lead to sudden settlement or collapse during erection. Work must be halted and corrected before continuing.",
  "references": [
    "AISC 303 - Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges (base plate bearing, shimming, and leveling requirements)",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R - Steel Erection (column stability and erection safety requirements)"
  ]
}
